title: Ingest Callan-Harvey And Jackiw-Rebbi For The Edge-Mode Proof
summary: Return to L0 only for denser equation-level archival mapping of Callan-Harvey and Jackiw-Rebbi and for the broader continuum-to-lattice bridge; the core Lecture Three continuum theorem family is source-grade locally and now includes an explicit half-level theorem, hemisphere-Berry-flux fragment, Callan-Harvey cancellation fragment, and lecture-one boundary precursor.
assumptions: The Witten-based wall-mode theorem and parity-anomaly spine are already present locally.
Dedicated Jackiw-Rebbi and anomaly-inflow theorems are already fused into the canonical family.
The remaining work is finer archival detail and broader neighboring-source linkage.
scope: L0 -> L1 -> L3 -> L2 task packet for denser archival recovery of the continuum edge-mode derivation and anomaly inflow.

source_anchors.notes: Triggered now by the need for denser archival mapping and broader neighboring-source bridges beyond the already explicit proof chain.

candidate_sources.citation: C. G. Callan Jr. and J. A. Harvey, "Anomalies And Fermion Zero-Modes On Strings And Domain Walls," Nucl. Phys. B250 (1985) 427.
candidate_sources.why: Witten cites anomaly inflow as the boundary-consistency mechanism.
